clinical_note: Patient is a 47-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He has been noted to have chills, fever, malaise, and fatigue. His white blood cell count is elevated at 21.6, with a band count of 4 and neutrophils at 86%. During his stay of about 9 days, he underwent several procedures including arterial catheterization, endoscopic biliary stent placement, mechanical ventilation, central venous catheter placement, and hemodialysis.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 58-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 8 days. He presented with symptoms including chills and fatigue. During his hospitalization, he underwent several procedures including an internal mammary coronary artery bypass, multivessel coronary artery bypass, and cardiopulmonary bypass. His white blood cell count was noted to be 13.7.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 79-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital for further evaluation. He experienced chills, fatigue, fever, and malaise during his stay, which lasted about 6 days. Laboratory results showed a white blood cell count of 12.6, with neutrophils comprising 82.9% of the total. The patient underwent percutaneous abdominal drainage and received parenteral infusion of concentrated nutritional substances.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 52-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 2 days. During his stay, he underwent several procedures including venous catheterization, percutaneous abdominal drainage, and the placement of an intra-abdominal venous shunt. His white blood cell count was noted to be 16.5, with a band count of 6 and neutrophils comprising 82% of the total white cell count.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 66-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 9 days. He presented with chills and fever. Laboratory findings revealed a white blood cell count of 14.9 and neutrophils at 83.9%. He was started on IV and oral antibiotics.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 82-year-old female was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. During her stay of about 2 days, her white blood cell count was noted at 9.5.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 81-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital and had a length of stay of about 7 days. During this time, he exhibited chills, fatigue, and fever. His white blood cell count was elevated at 36.8, with a band count of 10 and neutrophil percentage at 81. He underwent several procedures, including measurement of cardiac sampling and pressure in both the left and right heart, dilation of a coronary artery with a drug-eluting device, and assistance with cardiac output utilizing a balloon pump. Additionally, he received irrigation of the genitourinary tract and fluoroscopy of multiple coronary arteries. The patient was started on broad antibiotic cover, administered both orally and intravenously.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 44-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He has been hospitalized for about 10 days. During the stay, cardiac output was continuously monitored, and a bypass was performed on one artery from the left internal mammary. The white blood cell count was noted at 15.7, with neutrophils making up 64%.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: This 77-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. Upon admission, he exhibited altered mental status, fatigue, fever, and rigors. His white blood cell count was noted to be 8.8. A right heart cardiac catheterization was performed as part of the clinical evaluation. He has been hospitalized for about 9 days.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 63-year-old female was urgently admitted following transfer from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 14 days. Interventions included control of epistaxis through anterior nasal packing, percutaneous abdominal drainage, and laryngoscopy with other tracheoscopy. She required mechanical ventilation and venous catheterization during her stay. Laboratory values noted a white blood cell count of 11.4, with a neutrophil percentage of 83 and a band count of 3.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 56-year-old male who was urgently admitted following transfer from another hospital. He demonstrated notable clinical signs including low blood pressure and a rapid heart rate. Throughout his approximately 45-day hospital stay, he underwent several significant procedures, which included other electroshock therapy, insertion of an endotracheal tube, and mechanical ventilation. A central venous catheter was also placed to facilitate care. Nutritional support was provided via both parenteral and enteral infusion of concentrated nutritional substances. Laboratory findings indicated a white blood cell count of 10 and a neutrophil percentage of 81.5.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 44-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. His white blood cell count was 7.2, with neutrophils accounting for about 68%. The length of stay was approximately 3 days.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 60-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, remaining for about 14 days. He presented with chills, cough, and malaise. Laboratory results indicated a white blood cell count of 52.8 and a CRP of 38.5. He showed a neutrophil percentage of 51 and a band count of 1. He was started on IV antibiotics.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: Urgently admitted from another hospital, a 34-year-old male stayed for about 11 days. During his hospital course, he underwent several procedures including repositioning of the right mandible with an internal fixation device, repositioning of the left maxilla and left mandible with external fixation devices, and repair of the upper artery. He had a white blood cell count of 16.2.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 46-year-old female who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 17 days. She presented with fever and shortness of breath. During her stay, her white blood cell count was recorded at 14.2, with neutrophils comprising 67% of the total. Various procedures were performed including inspection of the upper intestinal tract, intermittent urinary filtration for less than 6 hours per day, introduction of nutritional substance into the lower GI tract, drainage of the peritoneal cavity, insertion of a feeding device into the jejunum, and insertion of an infusion device into the right atrium. She was started on IV and oral antibiotics.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: Patient is a 53-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from a skilled nursing facility. He had a length of stay of about 4 days. During hospitalization, an intramedullary internal fixation device was inserted into the left upper femur. Additionally, intermittent urinary filtration was performed for less than 6 hours per day. Laboratory values showed a white blood cell count of 11.4 with neutrophils at 72.9%.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 86-year-old female was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. She presented with altered mental status. Laboratory findings revealed a white blood cell count of 41.1 and a neutrophil percentage of 92.6. The length of stay was about 4 days.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 78-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He has been in the hospital for about 8 days. His white blood cell count is 9.9, with neutrophils making up 68.9% of the total. He is receiving IV antibiotics.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 48-year-old female was urgently admitted following transfer from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 11 days. She presented with skin discoloration and warmth. During her stay, her white blood cell count was noted at 8.2.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 52-year-old female who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. She has a white blood cell count of 4.2. During her short stay of about 1 day, she underwent a percutaneous abdominal drainage procedure.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 55-year-old female was urgently admitted following transfer from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 8 days. She presented with chills and had a white blood cell count of 15.7, with neutrophils accounting for 69% of the total. She received IV and oral antibiotics during her stay. A band count of 2 was also noted.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 87-year-old male was admitted urgently after transfer from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 5 days. During his hospitalization, he underwent several procedures including mechanical ventilation, fluoroscopy of the inferior mesenteric artery, inspection of the upper intestinal tract, extirpation of matter from the stomach, fluoroscopy of the superior mesenteric artery, and insertion of an infusion device into the superior vena cava. He was noted to have chills and fatigue, with a white blood cell count of 20.9 and a neutrophil percentage of 71.6.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 72-year-old female who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. During her 30-day stay, she exhibited symptoms including chills, fatigue, and fluid-filled blisters. Laboratory results showed a white blood cell count of 11.5 with a neutrophil percentage of 77, and CRP was elevated at 156.2. Documented procedures included the insertion of an endotracheal airway, fluoroscopy of the left kidney, ureter, and bladder, mechanical ventilation, ultrasonography of the superior vena cava, drainage of the right pleural cavity, and insertion of an infusion device into the superior vena cava. The patient was treated with both IV and oral/NG antibiotics.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: An 88-year-old female was urgently admitted following transfer from another hospital. She presented with fatigue and fever. Initial lab work demonstrated a white blood cell count of 13.6 and a neutrophil percentage of 86. The length of stay was about 2 days.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: 70-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He presented with vomiting and had a white blood cell count of 14.1 and a CRP of 267.9. His neutrophil percentage was elevated at 93, and the band count was noted to be 2. The length of stay was about 6 days.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 53-year-old male was urgently admitted, having been transferred from another hospital. He has been hospitalized for about 5 days. Upon admission, the white blood cell count was 14.1. No specific symptoms were documented.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 60-year-old male was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He has been noted to have sputum production. White blood cell count was 16.3 with a band count of 30 and neutrophils at 70%. During his stay, he underwent enteral infusion of concentrated nutritional substances and arterial catheterization, along with prolonged mechanical ventilation. The length of stay was about 5 days.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 70-year-old male who underwent urgent admission following transfer from another hospital. He has experienced fatigue throughout his stay. Notable procedures included cardiopulmonary bypass, three-vessel coronary artery bypass, and internal mammary coronary artery bypass. Laboratory evaluation revealed a white blood cell count of 19.4, with neutrophils comprising 73.6%.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 53-year-old female was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 22 days. She presented with symptoms including chills, fatigue, sputum production, and watery diarrhea. Initial laboratory findings indicated a white blood cell count of 25.6, with neutrophils comprising 90.5% of the differential. She was started on IV antibiotics as part of her treatment regimen.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 48-year-old female who was urgently admitted after an internal transfer from a psychiatric unit. She spent about 7 days in the hospital. During her stay, laboratory evaluations revealed a white blood cell count of 21.6 and neutrophils at 86%. Broad antibiotic coverage was initiated based on her clinical presentation. No additional symptoms were documented.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 51-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital. He experienced malaise during his stay, which lasted about 23 days. A fiber-optic bronchoscopy and colonoscopy were performed, along with a closed biopsy of the kidney. He required mechanical ventilation and had a central venous catheter placed. The patient was noted to have a white blood cell count of 10.2 and CRP of 135.1, with neutrophils at 81.8%. He was continued on IV antibiotics and received enteral infusion of concentrated nutritional substances.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 62-year-old male who was urgently admitted after being transferred from another hospital, with a length of stay of about 8 days. He presented with fatigue and fever. During the hospitalization, he underwent several procedures including continuous cardiac output monitoring, excision of the right saphenous vein, and bypass surgery involving one artery from the left internal mammary and two arteries from the coronary artery. His white blood cell count was recorded at 13.6, with neutrophils comprising 79%.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: This is a 63-year-old female who was electively admitted following a physician referral, with a length of stay of about 3 days. The patient underwent an excision of a dental lesion of the jaw and extraction of another tooth during her admission. She presented with flank pain, suprapubic tenderness, and foul odor from the incision site. Laboratory results indicated a white blood cell count of 12.3 with a neutrophil percentage of 70.4.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 63-year-old female was electively admitted following a physician referral. She underwent other operations on carotid body, carotid sinus, and other vascular bodies, as well as the implantation or replacement of a carotid sinus stimulation device, total system. Her white blood cell count was noted at 9.9 during the hospital stay, which lasted about 5 days.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 43-year-old male who was electively admitted upon physician referral, with a length of stay of about 9 days. During this admission, he underwent multiple procedures including placement of a central venous catheter, lysis of peritoneal adhesions, an open right hemicolectomy, and destruction of a liver lesion. Throughout his stay, the patient experienced symptoms such as fatigue, malaise, along with localized redness and warmth. His white blood cell count was recorded at 9.3.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 70-year-old male who was electively admitted following a physician referral. He experienced chills and fatigue during his hospital stay, which lasted about 5 days. A white blood cell count was noted at 9.7, and the patient was continued on IV antibiotics.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 63-year-old male was electively admitted following a physician referral. Over a stay of about 24 days, he presented with fatigue and malaise. Lab results indicated a white blood cell count of 21.1, with a neutrophil percentage of 94 and a band count of 4. He was started on oral antibiotics and continued on antibiotics via NG tube.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 76-year-old male who was electively admitted following a physician referral. He underwent a thoracoscopic lobectomy of the lung and a regional lymph node excision during his stay, which lasted about 7 days. Laboratory results showed a white blood cell count of 11.4 and a neutrophil percentage of 81.3.
label: Low Infection Concern

clinical_note: The patient is a 65-year-old female who was electively admitted on referral. She experienced chills, fatigue, fever, and malaise during her stay. Her white blood cell count rose significantly to 182.6, with a band count of 7 and neutrophils at 55%. She was started on IV antibiotics. The length of stay was about 17 days.
label: High Infection Concern

clinical_note: A 52-year-old male was electively admitted upon physician referral for a scheduled procedure. Laboratory evaluations indicated a white blood cell count of 13.5.
label: Low Infection Concern
